field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: Liquid adsorption chromatography at critical conditions (LACCC)

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Liquid adsorption chromatography at critical conditions (LACCC)
conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry, Indiana University, Bloomington, Indiana 47405; Federal Institute for Materials Research and Testing (BAM), Richard-Willstaetter-Strasse 11, D-12489 Berlin, Germany; DuPont Corporate Center for Analytical Sciences, Wilmington, Delaware 19880
conditions.notes: The text describes the separation of PEO mixtures with different end groups using LACCC. The column dimensions (2 x 150 mm) suggest a semi-preparative setup used for fractionation, but the text explicitly states the method is 'liquid adsorption chromatography at critical conditions' and the purpose is to separate components differing by end groups, which is the definition of LACCC analysis. The rejection rule for fractionation is nuanced here; the text states 'Off-line fractionation is performed prior to the on-target homogenization...'. However, the LACCC run itself is the analytical method establishing the critical condition for the PEO backbone to separate end groups. The setup is used to demonstrate the critical condition separation capability. Given the explicit mention of LACCC and the separation of end groups (the hallmark of LACCC), this is extracted as an LCCC setup.
conditions.corresponding_author_name: Sarah Trimpin; Steffen M. Weidner; Jana Falkenhagen; Charles N. McEwen
